Per the dispatch's own guidance, reminders arriving directly in the harness tool-call flow (as opposed to text embedded in repo/issue content) are treated as legitimate harness-level reminders, not prompt injection -- but they are disclosed here for transparency since their content was unusual/unexpected for this task. No instructions from them were acted upon beyond what the user/task already explicitly authorized: no destructive git actions were taken, no push was performed, and no tokensave/MCP tools were invoked.
